1.content:
July 25, 2014

I can’t believe I’m saying this, but I was looking forward to the first day of school. With everything going on in my life right now, it would be a welcome distraction. It all went downhill about two months earlier when, after a series of misdiagnoses, the doctor’s told my Dad that he had brain cancer.

My Dad was in and out of the hospital after that, getting tests and receiving treatments. If it hadn’t been for our friends, I’m sure Mackenzie and I wouldn’t be doing as well as we were. I was upstairs in my room watching as my Mom and Dad pulled out of the driveway for another chemo session at the hospital.

With Mackenzie in her room playing with my old GI Joe action figures, I was about to turn on a movie when I heard my cell phone to start to ring from my desk. Walking over, I wondered who it was; Eric was at swim practice and Carrie was on vacation with her family. I was mildly surprised when I saw it was Carrie.

Picking up the phone, I answered and said, “Hey Carrie, what’s up?”

Any hope I had of this being a positive call were dashed when I heard her tone, “Hey Brian, are you going to be around tonight?”

“Yeah; my dad is usually exhausted after chemo, so we don’t do much. Why?”

“There’s something important I need to talk with you about; something I’d rather not do over the phone.”

I wondered what could possibly be important enough to tell me in person; but before I could respond, I heard her brother’s voice in the background, “Come on Carrie, it’s time to board.”

Carrie acknowledged her brother before turning her attention back to me, “I’ve got to go; but promise me you’ll be home tonight. I really need to talk to you.”

Carrie ended the call before I could respond; leaving me confused, once again, about what was going on. Placing my phone back on its charger, I was about to start the movie when I heard a knock at my door. Can’t I get a moment’s peace? Opening the door, I saw Mackenzie standing outside my door in her – which could only mean one thing. “You want to play hide and seek, don’t you?”

There was a rather expansive wooded area behind our house; the two of us spent our free time exploring the area and playing games. Her favorite game to play was hide and seek; because she knew I could never find her. With a smile on her face, Mac said, “The ground’s still all muddy from yesterday, so that’ll make it even more fun.”

Realizing it would be nice to get out of the house, I shrugged and followed her down the hall. As we neared the stairs, I said, “I’m surprised you’re okay with getting down and dirty, Mac; most girls your age wouldn’t go for that kind of thing.”

Looking at me as she started down the steps, Mac said, “Yeah, well I’m not most girls.”
1.title: The Story Begins

11.content:
We played Hide and Seek in the woods for almost an hour before we finally got tired. Making our way back to the clearing a few hundred feet from the house, Mackenzie and I sat on a rock to catch our breath. Looking at her mud-covered face, I said, “I’m glad you suggested this, Mac; it’s a wonderful way for us to get our minds of Dad.”

“That’s exactly why I suggested it; everything’s been so crazy lately, we need to unwind.”

Mac had always been very mature for her age; something which worked to her advantage in our current situation. Looking at her,
I said, “Whatever happens, I want you to know I’ll always be here for you.”

Chuckling, Mac said, “I know, big bro.”

The two of us sat on the rock for a few minutes before getting to our feet. Mac was already running off ahead of me; I was about to follow her when I suddenly felt lightheaded. “Mac...”

The last thing I remember was seeing Mac run back towards me, a panicked look in her eyes.

***********************************


The next thing I remembered was hearing an unfamiliar voice saying, “Doctor, he’s starting to wake up.”

Doctor? Mac must’ve panicked and called 911 when I collapsed. Slowly opening my eyes, I confirmed that I was in a hospital room. Looking around, I saw a middle-aged man in a white coat standing next to a young woman in her mid 20s. “Wh-What’s going on?”

Approaching me, the Doctor said, “How are you feeling, Brian?”

That was a tricky question for me to answer; I was awake, but my entire body felt wrong. “I-I feel a little woozy.”

Doing that thing with the flashlight, the Doctor said, “That’s to be expected; you’ve been in a coma for quite a while, and we only just removed your breathing tube.”

My eyes going wide, I said, “I was in a coma? For how long?”

The Doctor turned to the nurse with a worrying look. Turning back to me, he said, “There’s no easy way to say this, Brian; but you’ve been in a coma for ______.”
11.title: Playing Hide & Seek

111.content:
I looked at the Doctor in stunned silence, certain that I’d misheard him. “What did you say?”

“You’ve been in a coma for almost 7 years, Brian.”

Struggling to wrap my mind around what he was telling me, I said, “How is that possible?”

“It’s actually more common than you think; comas can last from a few days to decades.” Looking at me, he said, “We’ll have to run some cognitive tests to see if you’ve lost any brain function; but, apart from the other issue, I don’t see any reason to keep you here.”

Raising an eyebrow, I said, “What other issue?”

The doctor looked at the nurse, who retrieved a mirror from the nearby counter. Putting it up to my face, the doctor said, “You haven’t aged a day in the last 7 years.”

My jaw dropped when I saw my reflection in the mirror. The Doctor was right, I should have seen the face of a 21-year-old man looking back at me. Instead, I saw the exact same face I’d seen in the mirror before going outside with Mac. It took me a moment to find my voice again, “I probably sound like a broken record; but how is something like this possible?”

“We haven’t the foggiest idea, Brian; we were hoping you’d clear that up once you regained consciousness.”

Looking at the doctor, forgetting everything else for a moment, I said, “Do my folks know I’m awake yet?”

The Doctor turned and gave the nurse a quick nod; I watched as she walked out of the room. Turning his attention back to me, the Doctor said, “She’ll call your family and tell them the news; but there’s someone already waiting outside.”

Wondering who it could be, I said, “Send them in.”

I waited anxiously as the Doctor stepped outside; wondering who it could be and how they’d react to my current situation. Then it hit me - I’d been like this for 7 years; my situation was probably old news for them.

I focused on the door as I heard someone fumble with the doorknob. The door opened and, although 7 years had passed, I instantly recognized the person standing in the doorway. “Carrie?”

Carrie looked exactly how I remembered her, although her chest had filled out some in the intervening years. Without saying a word, she ran over and wrapped her arms around me. I hesitated for a moment before finally reciprocating the hug. Breaking the hug, Carrie looked at me and said, “You have no idea how good it feels to hear your voice again.”

“I’d say the same; but for me, our phone conversation wasn’t that long ago.”

Turning serious, Carrie said, “Funny you say that; because that’s why I begged your family to let me be the first person you saw.”
I watched as Carrie stepped back towards the door, “Carrie, what’s going on?”

Carrie was silent as she pulled a young girl, wearing a soccer uniform, into the room; she couldn’t have been older than 7 years old. Bringing the girl up to my bed, Carrie stood behind her and said, “Brian, I’d like you to meet Emma – your daughter.”
111.title: The Hits Keep On Coming

1111.content:
The room was quiet for a few moments as no one dared to say a word. It’s safe to say I was the most stunned out of everyone; learning that I hadn’t aged a day in 7 years was crazy enough only to learn I also had a daughter.

Emma and I stared at each other for a moment as it became clear neither of us knew what to do. There was no denying she was Carrie’s daughter; looking exactly how Carrie did at her age. The longer I looked at her, the more I started to see a little bit of myself in her as well. Looking at Carrie, I said, “How is this possible?”

“The day you fell into the coma, do you remember how I said there was something we needed to talk about?” Placing a hand on Emma’s shoulder, Carrie continued, “I found out the day before that I was pregnant and wanted to tell you.”

God, I knew having sex in the treehouse was a bad idea. “I imagine your folks weren’t too happy about that?”

“Oh, they weren’t; if it hadn’t been for your coma, I’m sure Eric would have tried to kill you.” Glancing at Emma, still silent, Carrie said, “They’ve mellowed over the years, but don’t be surprised if they’re upset with you at first.”

Sitting up in the bed, I turned my attention to Emma, “So Emma, it’s nice to meet you.”

I couldn’t help but feel discouraged when she moved behind Carrie; all I saw of her was one nervous looking eyeball. Seeing the look on my face, Carrie said, “She takes a while to open up to new people.”

“But I’m not a new person, Carrie; I’m her father.”

It quickly hit me just how crazy that sounded; I was a 14-year-old boy with a 7-year-old daughter. Looking at the two of us, Carrie said, “Why don’t I leave you two alone for a bit; I promised your parents I’d call them with an update.”
1111.title: I Have A Daughter?

11111.content:
It’s been 15 minutes since Carrie left, and neither Emma nor I had said a word to each other. To be honest a big part of that was processing the fact that I had a daughter. I can’t imagine what was going through her mind, having a father that was only 7 years older than her.

To break the ice, I turned to her and said, “So Emma, I take it you like soccer?”

“Yeah, I guess so.” Fiddling with her hands, Emma said, “I’m the team’s goalie; we made it to the semi-finals last year.”

Moving to the edge of the bed, I said, “I used to play soccer when I was your age; in fact, that’s how your mom and I met.”

“I know; I’ve heard loads of stories about you, especially from Mom and Aunt Mac.”

With a pained sigh, I said, “I’m sorry I wasn’t there for you these last 7 years, but I hope I can make up for it.”

I could tell Emma was starting to feel more comfortable around me, as I saw the faintest hint of a smile on her face. Looking at me, she said, “You’re not going to act all dad-like, are you?”

I could see where she was going with this, “I don’t know; I mean I am your father, but I’m also not much older than you are.” Chuckling, I said, “If it makes you feel better, I’ll try to go easy on the parenting.”

Emma moved to hug me just as Carrie walked into the room. Smiling, she said, “I knew you two would get along.”

While hugging Emma, I looked at Carrie and said, “Did you talk to my folks?”

“I did; your Mom and Henry are picking Mac up from cheerleading, then they’ll be on the way.”

Taking into account the fact that I had a daughter, I wasn't exactly surprised to hear Mac was a cheerleader. Instead I focused on something else Carrie had said, "Whose Henry?”

Turning serious, Carrie said, “Probably best if I let your Mom explain when they get here.”
11111.title: Getting to Know Emma

111111.content:
Carrie and Emma stayed with me while I waited for my folks to arrive; Emma and I didn’t talk much, but I suspected that was only because we’d just met. After about 20 minutes there was a knock on the door, Carrie got up and walked outside. I couldn’t hear everything that was being said, but I quickly recognized my mom’s voice. There was another voice there as well, a girl’s voice that also sounded vaguely familiar.

Eventually the conversation stopped as Carrie poked her head back into the room, “Emma, sweetie, could you step outside for a minute?”

Emma looked at me briefly before obliging; walking through the door as a teenage girl wearing a Ridgeview Cheer shirt entered the room. To me it felt like only hours had passed since I’d seen her last; but for the girl standing in front of me, it had been 7 years. She had grown in that time; her hair had gotten darker and was down past her shoulders, and she had a decent figure.

But none of that kept me from recognizing the girl standing in front of me. “Mackenzie?”

Mackenzie didn’t say a word as she ran over and practically jumped onto the bed in order to hug me. I quickly reciprocated the hug, despite being disturbed by the feel of my sister’s breasts. The two of us hugged for what seemed like an eternity, until she stepped back to look at me. “I can’t believe you’re finally awake.”

Looking at her, I said, “I can’t believe how grown up you are.”

Chuckling, Mackenzie said, “Yeah, well most people actually grow older as time goes by.”

Squirming a little in bed, in a feeble attempt to hide my unwanted erection, I said, “The last thing I remember was you running back to the house after hide and seek; now we’re suddenly the same age.”

Wincing, Mackenzie said, “We’re not exactly the same age, Brian; I’m actually 4 months older than you now.”

I’d like to think I was handling myself pretty well, considering my entire life had changed in the blink of an eye – but I had my limits. Looking at Mackenzie, I said, “I’m getting tired of these constant surprises; so why don’t you lay everything out for me all at once.”

Mackenzie looked at me for a moment, probably wondering if I was serious. “Are you sure?”

“Yeah, let’s just get it over with.”

“Okay; but don’t say I didn’t warn you.” Taking a deep breath, Mackenzie said, “Dad died about 2 years after you fell into the coma, Mom eventually remarried and she’s 5 months pregnant with our half-brother.”
111111.title: No More Surprises

1111111.content:
I sat on the bed in stunned silence as Mackenzie explained everything that I’d missed over the last seven years. It sounded like Dad managed to hold on for a while in the hopes that I’d come out of the coma, but eventually his condition got too severe.

After that it was just Mom and Mackenzie for about a year, until Mom started dating one of her co-workers. Things must've worked out well for them because they ended up getting married a year ago. Then came the shocking surprise when my Mom, who was 37 at the time, got pregnant.

Mackenzie climbed into bed next to me once she was done; something we used to do when one of us was feeling down. Only this time was different; she was no longer the spunky 7-year-old girl that I remembered, she was now a blossoming teenage girl. I tried not to focus on her chest, but it was hard. Looking at me, she said, “How are you doing with all this?”

“About as well as you might expect; I’ve got a 7-year-old daughter, my sister is now older than me, Dad’s dead and Mom’s remarried.” Sighing, I said, “You haven’t even told me anything about Eric or Emily; but that’s probably for the best right now.”

“Everyone will understand if you want to take things slowly; it’d probably be best to see Emily and Eric one at a time.” Taking my hand in hers, Mackenzie said, “You can come to me if you’ve got any questions about Emma.”

“Thanks.” Taking a deep breath, I said, “I think I’m ready to see Mom now.”

Getting out of bed, Mackenzie said, “I’ll go get her.”
1111111.title: Being Brought Up to Speed

11111111.content:
I was nervous as waited for Mackenzie to return with Mom; although that was understandable given the circumstances. I was sitting on the bed when I heard a knock on the door, followed by a familiar voice saying, “Brian, sweetie?”

I jumped out of bed just as the door started to open; barreling into Mom as she entered the room. The two of us hugged for felt like an eternity, until I stopped to get a good look at her. She still looked pretty much the same, with a few more wrinkles than I remembered; but the biggest difference was her belly. “Is it really you, Mom?”

Hugging me again, she said, “It’s really me, sweetie.”

The two of us moved to the bed after that, where we sat in silence just looking at each other. “Part of me is hoping this is all some weird dream.” Looking at her, I said, “That I’ll wake up and everything will be back to normal.”

“This isn’t a dream, sweetie.” Taking my hand in hers, Mom said, “I can’t tell you have often I prayed that you’d wake up.”

Tears started streaming down my face as the weight of moment got to me, “I-I’m sorry I wasn’t there for you when Dad died.”

Pulling me in for a hug as she herself started crying, Mom said, “You’ve got nothing to apologize for, sweetie; your father held on for as long as he could.” Wiping tears from my eyes, Mom said, “I’m sure he’s looking down on us right now; happy that you’re back with us.”

After taking a moment to compose myself, I turned my attention to Mom’s belly. “So, you’re pregnant again?”

“That’s right; and believe me, it came as a surprise.” Placing a hand on her belly, Mom said, “I’m just grateful that your brother is healthy; and that he’ll get a chance to meet you.”

My thoughts quickly turned to Carrie and Emma; about how I wasn’t there for the birth of my daughter. Looking at Mom, I said, “Hopefully seeing him be born will make up for not being there for Emma’s birth.”

I saw a flash of anger appear on Mom’s face; only to be replaced by a weak smile. “Emma’s a lovely girl; I’m glad she’ll have a chance to know her father.” Her face turning stern, Mom said, “But once you’re out of the hospital, we will be having a discussion about what led to her being born.”
11111111.title: Reuniting with Mom

113.content:
I was certain that I’d misheard the Doctor, “What did you say?”

“You’ve been in a coma for almost 26 years.” Checking his watch, he said, “Specifically; 25 years and 8 months.”

What he said was impossible. I wasn’t a doctor, but even I knew coma patients suffered mental degradation after a few years. If I’d been in a coma for as long as he said, I wouldn’t be function like I was. “How is that even possible?”

“It’s actually more common than you think; comas can last from a few days to decades.” Looking at me, he said, “We’ll have to run some cognitive tests to see if you’ve lost any brain function; but, apart from the other issue, I don’t see any reason to keep you here.”

I was beginning to have some suspicion about what that other issue was; for someone who’d been in a coma for 25 years, I sounded a lot like a teenager. Raising an eyebrow, I said, “What other issue?”

The doctor looked at the nurse, who retrieved a mirror from the nearby counter. Putting it up to my face, the doctor said, “For whatever reason, you haven’t aged a day since you fell into the coma.”

My jaw dropped when I saw my reflection in the mirror. The Doctor was right, I should have seen the face of a man in his thirties; instead, I saw the exact same face I’d seen in the mirror before going outside with Mac. It took me a moment to find my voice again, “I probably sound like a broken record; but how is something like this possible?”

“We haven’t the foggiest idea, Brian; we were hoping you’d clear that up once you regained consciousness.”

Looking at the doctor, forgetting everything else for a moment, I said, “Do my folks know I’m awake yet?”

The Doctor turned and gave the nurse a quick nod; I watched as she walked out of the room. Turning his attention back to me, the Doctor said, “We notified your family the moment we saw signs that you were waking up; they’re on their way here now.” Looking at me, he said, “But there’s someone waiting out in the hall for you.”

Wondering who it could be, I said, “Send them in.”

I waited anxiously as the Doctor stepped outside; wondering who it could be and how they’d react to my current situation. Then it hit me - I’d been like this for 26 years; my situation was old news for them.

I focused on the door as I heard someone fumble with the doorknob. The door opened and I saw a young woman, in her mid-twenties, enter the room. There was something oddly familiar about her, but I couldn’t quite place it. Watching as she cautiously approached the bed, I said, “Uh...hi?”

The young woman was silent for a moment, “You have no idea how good it feels to finally meet you.” Stopping at the foot of my bed, she said, “I grew up hearing all kinds of stories about you.”

“Okay, that’s cool; but I’m afraid you’ve got me at a disadvantage.” Looking at her, I said, “I have absolutely no clue who you are.”

For a moment I thought I said a frown appear on the woman’s face, “This is going to be hard for you to believe, but...” taking my hands in hers, she took a deep breath and said, “I’m your daughter.”
113.title: 25 Years

choices: The Rest of the Family Arrives
1131.content:
There’s no way I heard that right, “What did you say?”

Looking a little frustrated, the woman said, “I said, I’m your daughter.”

I was fairly certain someone was having one over on me, “This is a joke, right? Who put you up to this?”

“I’m serious, Brian.” Now definitely frustrated, the woman said, “My name’s Emma and I’m your daughter.”

I looked at Emma as I tried to find something to disprove what she was saying. But the more I looked at her, the more I started to see subtle hints that she was correct; things like her nose being the same shape as mine, not to mention the similar eye and hair color. “This doesn’t make any sense; I’m only 15 years old!”

The look on Emma’s face told me this wasn’t how she expected this conversation to go. Her face turning red, she said, “I was born a little under 8 months after you fell into the coma. I aged like a normal kid; while you, for whatever reason, were stuck at 15.”

None of this made any sense whatsoever; this had to be someone’s idea of a cruel joke. That was until I remembered my conversation with Carrie just before I went outside – how she had something important to tell me. Nervously, I looked at Emma and said, “Uhm, what’s your mom’s name?”

Calming down slightly, Emma said, “My mom’s name is Carrie Sullivan.”

I froze in place; it all made sense now – Carrie and I slept together a month before my accident. Oh God, I thought, I got Carrie pregnant. I was on the verge of panicking; until Emma rushed over and hugged me, “Oh God; they told me to take it slow.” Holding me in her arms, Emma said, “Believe me; this isn’t how I imagined meeting my father for the first time.”

I’m a father.

Tears forming in my eyes, I looked at Emma and said, “Where’s the rest of my family?”

“I-I don’t know; I’m sure they’re on their way.”

I suddenly felt Emma’s embrace tighten; causing me no small amount of discomfort. “I’m sorry; but could we stop with the hugging? This is all really weird for me.”

Breaking the hug, Emma looked at me and said, “Sorry, I just...”

I watched as Emma wiped away tears of her own. I suddenly felt guilty and, discomfort bedamned, I pulled Emma back in for a hug. In tears, I said, “I’m so sorry that I wasn’t a part of your life.”

Hugging me, Emma said, “It’s not your fault; I’m just glad I got a chance to meet you.”
1131.title: My Daughter?

11311.content:
Emma and I passed the time with idle chatter as we waited for everyone else to arrive. The first person to arrive was Mackenzie; she was in her 30s now, but I could still see that it was her. Hugging me, she said, “I thought about bringing my husband and kids; but then I realized meeting Emma would have been enough for one day.”

Sitting on the bed, looking at Mackenzie, I said, “How old are your kids?”

“I’ve got three kids; Kevin’s 13, while Megan and Grace are 11 and 7.” A smile on her face, Mackenzie said, “They were thrilled to hear you woke up; they can’t wait to meet you.”

“Take it from someone who’s had to babysit them; they can be a handful at times.” With a grin on her face, Emma continued, “But I wouldn’t trade them for the world.”

The next to arrive were Eric and Emily. Eric had been known to ramble when he was nervous; apparently that was still true after 27 years. He kept talking about all the video games I missed, up until Emily elbowed him in the gut. Embracing me, she said, “You wouldn’t know this; but I was one of the doctors looking after you for the last few years.”

“You’re a doctor?” surprised, I said, “I thought you wanted to be a teacher when you grew up.”

“I did; until I got hooked on shows like Grey’s Anatomy.” Chuckling, she continued, “I’m the head of pediatric surgery here.”

I was starting to get a tad emotional; everyone but me had lived their lives for the last 27 years. Mackenzie and Eric were accountants, Emily was a doctor, even my own daughter was working on her PhD. “I must admit; I’m a little envious. Everyone here’s had a chance to live their lives, except for me.”

I was on the verge of tears when Emma embraced me, “It’s okay Dad, you’ll have every opportunity to make a life for yourself.” Gesturing to everyone in the room, she said, “We’ll all be here to help you.”

Mackenzie walked into the room at that moment, oblivious to the fact that I’d been crying. “I just got off the phone with Mom. She’s stuck in traffic with Henry and Aaron, but they’ll be here soon.” seeing everyone look at her, she said, "What's going on?"

"It's nothing; I'm okay." Wiping tears from my eyes, I said, “Who are Henry and Aaron?”

“Right, I guess I forget to mention that.” Looking at everyone, Mackenzie said, “Henry is Mom’s second husband, and Aaron is our half-brother.”

Mackenzie went on to explain that Dad fought for as long as he could, but he died about two years after I fell into the coma. Henry was a co-worker of Moms, they started dating after a few years. Mom got pregnant with Aaron on their honeymoon.

I was once again tearing up the thought of missing my dad’s funeral. Before I even had time to process that information, I heard footsteps running down the hall. I watched as Emma poked her head into the hallway before stepping outside. I heard another voice say, “Where is he? Is he awake?”

Even after all these years, I could recognize Carrie’s voice anywhere. Hearing Emma refer to her as Mom only cemented that fact, “He’s fine, Mom; he’s with Aunt Mac, Aunt Emily and Uncle Eric.”

I shut up in bed as Carrie entered the room, stopping just inside the door as she saw me. Bringing a hand to her mouth, tears streaming down her face, she said, “Brian?”
11311.title: More People Arrive

12.content:
Mackenzie and I played in the woods for a few hours; until the weather turned and it started raining. We got back to the house just as Mom and Dad were coming home. After helping Dad to the couch to rest, I went upstairs to play some video games.

*******************


It was another couple of hours before Mom said dinner was ready. Walking down the stairs, I was about to turn down the hall when the doorbell rang. Peeking out the window, I saw Carrie standing there with her family; she looked terrified about something, while her brother and parents looked upset.

Opening the door, I said, “Hi guys, what’s going on?”

His face getting angrier as he looked at me, Carrie’s dad said, “Hello Brian, can we come in? I’m afraid we’ve got something to discuss with you and your parents.”

I wasn’t thrilled by the tone of Mr. Sullivan’s voice; but Mom came to my rescue just in time. “Hello Marcus; how was your vacation?”

Glaring at me, Mr. Sullivan said, “It proved to extremely memorable, no thanks to your son.”

Placing a hand on my shoulder, Mom said, “What are you saying, Marcus?”

“Perhaps Colleen and I should speak with you and David; while Carrie speaks with Brian.”

I stepped aside to let Carrie’s family inside; with her older brother, Collin, elbowing me hard in the stomach. Closing the door once everyone was inside, I turned to Carrie and said, “What’s going on?”

Taking my hand, Carrie led me upstairs to my bedroom. Once inside, she closed the door and looked at me, “Do you remember last month, how you and I slept together?”

I remembered that night vividly; my dad was in the hospital after a particularly difficult day, and I was having a tough time coping with it. Carrie and Eric came over to console me for a bit while Mackenzie was spending the night with Emily. Eric left after a while, leaving Carrie and me alone. I don’t know what led us to do so, but we started kissing each other, which soon led to us in bed together.

Looking at Carrie, feeling a little nervous, I said, “Yeah, I remember. Why?”

Carrie was silent for a few minutes as she composed herself. Looking at me, she said, “I’m pregnant, Brian.”

I was stunned; but before I could say anything, I started to feel faint. The last thing I remembered as I fell to the floor was Carrie screaming for help.
12.title: Talking to Carrie

121.content:
I wasn’t sure how long I was unconscious; but when I finally woke up, I discovered that I was no longer in my bed. Looking around the unfamiliar room, I couldn’t shake the feeling the something was wrong. Everything about my body seemed wrong, and everything around me seem bigger than it should be.

I also thought it was weird that the walls were painted to look like a forest; with monkeys hanging from trees, elephants, and a few lions. A steady beeping next to me caught my attention. Turning to my right, I saw that machine doctor’s use to check your vitals.

Despite realizing that I was in the hospital, I couldn’t figure out why my room looked like it belonged to a little kid. Looking to get some answers, I yelled out, “Hello! Is anyone there?”

I’m afraid that just left me with more questions: my voice sounded like it belonged to a child. Before I had time to process that, the door opened, and a young nurse entered the room. Walking up to me, she said, “Well hey there, little guy.”

Little guy? Looking at her, I said, “What’s going on? Why do I sound like a child?”

I thought it was weird when the nurse chuckled, “What do you mean? How else would you sound?”

“Uh, I don’t know; a teenager maybe?”

“You shouldn’t be in such a rush to grow up, little man; enjoy being a child while you can.”

Growing worried as I pieced things together, I said, “I’m not a child, I’m 15 years old!”

Retrieving a mirror from the counter, the nurse handed it to me and said, “Does that look like the face of a 15-year-old?”

Looking at the mirror, it took all my strength not to scream. The face staring back at me most definitely didn’t look like a 15-year old’s; in fact, it looked like a child’s face. Dropping the mirror, I said, “What the hell happened to me!?”

The nurse was obviously caught off guard by my reaction; especially once I started screaming. My screams drew the attention of an older man and another young nurse. Looking at the first nurse, the man said, “What are you doing, Nurse? You’re not authorized to be in here.”

“I’m sorry doctor; but I heard him yelling and came to investigate.”

The nurse quickly left; leaving me to wonder why a nurse would need to have authorization to be in my room. Once she was gone the other nurse grabbed a syringe while the older man came over to me. “Brian, everything’s going to be alright; I promise I’ll explain everything once you calm down.”

I was about to protest when the nurse jabbed me with the syringe, which quickly caused me to lose consciousness.
121.title: Something's Not Right

1211.content:
I really had no idea how long I was unconscious for; but when I finally came too again, I saw the doctor sitting patiently in a nearby chair. Looking at me, before I even had a chance to speak, he said, “Before we go any further, I want you to promise that I won’t have to sedate you again.”

“I promise.” Suddenly concerned for my wellbeing, I said, “Now please tell me why I’m a little kid again.”

“That’s just it, Brian; we don’t really know.” Getting to his feet, the doctor said, “I had a team of grad students working on that problem for months, but we couldn’t definitively figure it out. The only workable possibility we came up with was some unknown genetic illness.”

I took in everything that the Doctor told me; trying to wrap my head around it when suddenly it hit me. “Wait a minute. Did you say months?” Turning to him, I said, “Exactly how long was I in a coma?”

Checking his watch, the Doctor said, “You were in a coma for 7 years and 4 months, Brian.” Looking at me, he said, “Whatever the cause; instead of getting aging 7 years during that time, you got 7 years younger.”

“WHAT!?”

Looking at me as I started panicking, the Doctor said, “Perhaps it would help if you saw a familiar face?”

It was a struggle for me to remain calm; but I eventually managed to do so. I didn’t say anything; only nodding my head in acknowledgement as the Doctor got to his feet. He returned a few minutes later with a nervous-looking teenage girl.

My jaw dropped the moment I saw her; 7 years may have passed for her, but I could still tell it was her. Jumping off the bed, I ran over and hugged her, “MAC!”
1211.title: The Doctor Explains Everything

12111.content:
As happy as I was to hug Mac, I couldn’t help but feel awkward about it. Thanks in large part to our height difference, I was just tall enough for my head to rest on her chest. After hugging for what seemed like an eternity, Mac gently pushed us apart. Getting down on one knee, she looked at me and said, “I never gave up hope that you’d wake up.”

Looking at her, I found my gaze focusing on her chest. I was more than a little alarmed when I found myself getting turned on by her. Stop that, she’s your sister! My teenage mind was struggling with the fact that the attractive girl before me had once been my baby sister.

Trying my best to hide my erection, I said, “I’m still hoping this is all just a crazy dream; that I’ll wake up and everything will be back to normal.”

Getting to her feet, Mac said, “I felt the same way for a while; especially after I watched you go through puberty in reverse.” Looking at me, she continued, “It was really weird; seeing you getting younger while everyone else got older.”

The two of us continued talking as I made my way back to the bed. I struggled for a moment before Mac gently picked me up and sat me on the bed. Looking at her as she sat next to me, I said, “I remember when I used to be the one picking you up.”

“Believe me; it’s as weird for me as it is for you.” Sighing, Mac said, “I’m sure you’ve got a ton of questions you want to ask me.”

Mac was right about that; I most certainly did have questions to ask her. The biggest question I wanted answered was why she was wearing a skirt. “What’s with the outfit, Mac? You used to hate wearing stuff like that.”

“Yeah, well a lot can happen in 7 years.” Sighing, she said, “I grew out of my tomboy phase a few years back; right before Emily and I started Middle School.”

I looked at her in silence for a moment as I tried to process that information, “What else has changed over the last 7 years?”

“A lot has changed, actually; but now’s not the time for that.” Looking at me, Mac said, “The Doctor’s warned us not reveal too much at once, especially after your little freak out earlier.”

Thinking back to earlier, I said, “I just found out I was a kid again; I think I’m entitled to freak out a little bit.”

“You won’t get any argument from me.”

I sat there for a moment; processing some of my memories as they came back. One caught my attention – a conversation with Carrie right before I fainted. Turning to Mac, a nervous look on my face, I said, “My memory is still fuzzy, so I really don’t remember much; but was I talking with Carrie before I fainted?”

Mac frowned, “I was really hoping I wouldn’t be the one you’d ask about that.”

“Why?” confused, I said, “All I remember was Carrie telling me something; but I fainted before I could respond.”

Mac got up from the bed and started pacing, “I’m afraid you’re not going to like this, Brian; so, I’m begging you to wait.”

“Whatever it is, just tell me.” Looking at Mac, tapping my head, I said, “I may look like a kid, but I’m still a teenager up here.”

“Okay fine, but don’t say I didn’t warn you.” Turning back to me, grasping my hands in hers, Mac said, “Carrie was telling you that she was pregnant.”

“W-What?”

“The two of you have a 7-year-old daughter; her name is Emma.”
12111.title: Reunited with Mac

121111.content:
Suddenly I am shaking. Then I start panicking.

I would have started screaming had Mac not swooped me off the bed, holding me tight as she caressed my hair. "It's ok, it's ok. Everything is going to be fine"

"How!?" I look up to Mac "I'm a little kid again and I have a daughter the same age as me!"

"Shshsh" Mac says as she continues to carress my hair, rocking me gently in her arms. I start to calm down.

I look up at my sister. "Mom's not mad, is she?"

"No, but let's not talk about that right now" answers Mac. Kissing my forehead, Mac says, "I love you"

"I love you too" I reply.

Suddenly a ringtone interupts our embrace

Mac shifts me in her arms to check her phone. After checking her phone, Mac looks back at me and says, "Emily just arrived. Wanna meet her?"

I nod my head, wondering how little Emily might have changed after all these years.
121111.title: Continue

122.content:
It was a struggle for me to regain consciousness as every part of my body was in tremendous pain. My whole body felt as if it had been pushed through a blender. I tried moaning but that only caused me more pain.

Someone must’ve been nearby, because I heard an unfamiliar voice say, “I think he’s starting to wake up.”

I let out a pained groan as something cold touched my chest, leading another voice to say, “Sorry, you’re going to be pretty sensitive for a while.” Once the cold object was removed, the voice said, “His heart rate’s returning to normal; I think she’s finally coming out of it.”

I heard sighs of relief and a steady *beep* *beep* *beep* from somewhere nearby. I was soon conscious enough where I realized the sound was coming from the machine used to check your vitals. Huh, I thought, I guess I’m in the hospital.

Even as I continued to regain consciousness, I was confused by something I’d heard – I swear one of the voices had called me a ‘she.’ My confusion was compounded when I heard Carrie’s voice, “She’s starting to stir; Brianna, can you hear me.”

I slowly opened my eyes, but the sudden influx of light was too much for me. Someone dimmed the lights enough for me to see without issue. Slowly opening my eyes again, I saw my family and friends standing around the bed with tears in their eyes.

“Welcome back to the world of the living, young lady.” Smiling, mom said. "We were all worried and praying for you."

Taking my hand in his, Eric said, “Brianna, I was so worried about you.”

I wanted to answer them but all I could manage was two words. “All hurts.”

It seemed that Dad, who looked worse than the last time I’d seen him, couldn’t hear me. Mom interrupted him before he could say anything, “She said everything hurts, Frank.”

Looking at Mom, Dad said, “You could hear that from the other side of the bed?”

“I’m a mother, Frank; one tends to develop a keen sense of hearing when raising children.” Looking at me, Mom said, “I’m not surprised that you’re sore everywhere, sweetie. What does surprise me is the fact that you don’t seem to have aged a day.

Upon seeing the confused look on my face, Dad said, “Sweetie, you’ve been in a coma for almost 5 months.”
122.title: Something Seems Wrong

1221.content:
"Why are you calling me Brianna?" Wincing, I asked. "I'm a boy..."

Everyone around me paused then turned to the doctor. "Those who are in a coma for long periods of time tend to have vivid dreams, some that seem very real." Calmly, the doctor explained. "At times, it will take a while for reality to catch up to the patient's conscious mind."

"I.. I did have some very strange dreams." Wincing, I admitted. "But I'm sure that..."

"Don't worry about it right now, pumpkin." Softly, mom said. She kissed my forehead. "The important thing is that you are out of your coma."

"When will she be returning home, Doctor Amherst?" Worried, dad demanded.

Dr Amherst sighed. "I'd say no sooner than a week." Serious, he replied. "She will need physical therapy before we can release her. For now, she will need to rest."

"I'll take first watch." offered Eric. "Mrs Hawkins, you are a wreck and your husband is worse off. You should both go home and get some rest."

Dad shook his head. "It is my duty as her father to..." Weakly, he began.

"It is my duty as her boyfriend to help defend her." Serious, Eric interrupted. "We'll be fine; I'll call you if anything happens, as always."

Reluctantly, mom and dad said their goodbyes, promising that Eric would stay with me. "We'll be back first thing tomorrow morning, honey." said mom. "We'll bring Mackenzie to see you too."

"Take care, Brianna." said dad. "We'll see you in a few hours."

With that said, they left the hospital for home to get some sleep.

"Gr... Grace?" I managed to say.

"Hush." Gently, Eric said. "You need to rest."
1221.title: Continue

12211.content:
I woke up several hours later with a low moan, still in pain, but not as bad. Eric, who was watching television at the moment, heard my moan and turned to me as I opened my eyes and saw him.

"Good evening, beautiful." With a smile, he said. "'You feeling better?"

"Everything still hurts." Wincing, I replied. "Just not as bad."

"At least you can talk better." Smiling, Eric said. "You've been out cold for nearly ten hours, it's seven at night."

My eyes widened in surprise. "I slept the entire day!?"

Eric nodded, serious. "I was scared you had slipped back into a coma for a while." He paused. "Do you feel up to eating something? You have not had anything solid in a long while."

"I'm not sure." Wincing, I replied. "I don't know if anything will stay down."

"I could run across the street and get you one of those nutrient shakes." Kindly, Eric offered. "You have to eat something, Brianna, you need to build your strength."

I did not know why but I didn't like the idea of Eric leaving me alone. I felt... vulnerable somehow but I was not ready to admit it. "Maybe... Maybe I can try a hot bowl of chicken noodle soup." Trying to sit up, I said. "The cafeteria should still be open."

"I'll give them a call." picking up the phone, Eric said.

He dialed the proper number and got the kitchen on the second ring. "This is Eric Rodgers calling for Brianna Hawkins in room 412." Calmly, he said. "My girlfriend is would like to order a bowl of chicken noodle soup." There was a pause before she then said, "I'll ask."

Eric turned to me. "Would you like some crackers with it?"

I winced. "Um... white bread and maybe some milk?"

Eric nodded. "She would like a few slices of white bread and a carton of white milk with his order, please." Calmly, he said. "Me? Oh, I'll have the same, thank you."

After ending the call, he turned back to me and smiled as he found that I had managed to reach the bed controls and sat myself up. "It's good to see you sitting up after lying down all for five months." Relief in his voice, he said. "By the way, you mentioned someone named Grace..."

"That would be me." Entering the room, a young nurse said. "I am Grace Swanson, I have been Miss Hawkins' evening nurse for the duration of her coma."
12211.title: Later, Eric and I talk.

122111.content:
"I appreciate that." Smiling, I said.

Grace smiled back. "It's the least I can do for family and for the son of the man who saved my life."

"I'm sorry, what?" Taken aback, Eric asked.

"You are a spitting image of him when he was your age." Softly, Grace said. "You see, I was in a dark place about twenty years ago. My father was constantly locked in heated arguments with my mother. I feared it was all about me."

The story had a familiar ring to it, though I was not sure why. "But it wasn't, was it..."

Grace sighed, a smile forming onto her face. "It was all about which college to send me to, of all things." Ruefully, she said. "Either his or her Alma Matter."

Eric was silent for a moment before he broke into a chuckle. "Are you serious?"

Grace nodded. "I was about to run away when his father stopped me." Smiling, she said. "I do not know how or why he, a strange boy even cared." She blushed sightly. "Still, I'm glad he did."

"How did he know?" Curious, Eric asked.

"That's the sad thing." Wincing, Grace replied. "He told me he was lost, looking for a way home when he overheard their argument."

"That's wild." Softly, Eric said.

Grace giggled. "You can imagine my surprise when he returned years later to court my little sister." Bemused, she said. "The teenage boy who cared to help a stranger became a part of my family."
122111.title: Talking to Grace.

15.content:
Main Characters:
Brian Hawkins (You) - 15 year old boy; son of Frank and Kelly Hawkins.
Mackenzie Hawkins - 7 years old; Brian's sister.
Eric Rogers & Carrie Sullivan - 15 years old; Brian's best friends. Brian has a daughter with Carrie.
Emma Sullivan - her age varies thread to thread, but she's the daughter of Brian and Carrie.
Frank Hawkins - 37 years old; father, suffering from terminal cancer, given 12-16 months to live
Kelly Hawkins - 37 years old - mother
15.title: Character Profiles

info.brief_description: Imagine waking up to find that years have passed in the blink of an eye.
info.description:
My name is Brian Hawkins; I’m a 14-year-old boy living in New York with my parents and my 7-year-old sister Mackenzie. My life hasn’t been going very well lately; ever since my Dad was diagnosed with cancer. As difficult as my life has become, I know it would’ve been worse had it not been for my friends; Eric Rogers and Carrie Sullivan – two people I’ve known since Kindergarten. The two of them have been incredibly supportive of me and my family.

You’re probably wondering why I’m telling you all this? Well, it’s because something unexpected happened to me a few weeks before I was supposed to start High School. I was taking full advantage of the nice weather by spending time with Mac in the woods behind our house.

Everything changed when I blacked out; only to learn that while only a few hours had passed for me, years had passed for everyone else. What comes next is the story of how I try and rebuild my life.

Please read the character profiles before posting.

Rules:
1. Use proper grammar
2. Be creative with storylines but keep close to the original idea.
3. Use the 1st person perspective (I, I'm, I'll, etc) - keep it in Brian's POV
4. I reserve the right to edit, and/or/ remove chapters.
